entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; platform for democratizing recruitment commissions

entities.name: Democratizing Recruiter Commission
entities.context: Core thesis: empowering non-professionals to earn referral commissions traditionally held by recruiters

entities.name: Data Superiority
entities.context: Professional-grade data and workflows that enable anyone to act as a talent broker

entities.name: Shareable Data Superiority
entities.context: Product priority: data tools designed for sharing, not just internal recruiter efficiency

entities.name: Talent Broker
entities.context: Role any individual can assume when armed with professional-grade recruitment data

entities.name: Brokerage for Human Capital
entities.context: Platform model: treating human talent placement as a financial brokerage

entities.name: Transaction-Based Brokerage Model
entities.context: Business model measuring success by commission volume claimed by non-recruiters

entities.name: Information Monopoly
entities.context: Traditional recruiter advantage that centralized intermediaries hold

entities.name: DeFi
entities.context: Analogous structural trend in finance: removing gatekeepers by distributing tools

entities.name: Creator Platforms
entities.context: Analogous structural trend in media: removing gatekeepers by arming participants

relationships.context: Core product thesis driving platform development
Platform provides professional-grade data to non-professionals
Platform conceived as financial instrument for talent placement
Success measured by non-recruiter commission volume, not subscription fees
Product priority is shareability over internal efficiency
Follows same structural pattern of removing centralized intermediaries
Follows same structural pattern of arming participants with gatekeeper advantages
Commission democratization works by commoditizing the recruiter's information advantage
